title: serapias lingua subsp. elsae
intro: Serapias lingua subsp. elsae, also known as the Tongue Orchid, is a rare sub-species of the Serapias lingua plant, a type of orchid that belongs to the Asphodelaceae family. Traditionally, the Serapias lingua subsp. elsae plant has been used in herbal medicine, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's medicinal properties are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, soothe sore throats, and aid in the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and pneumonia. Herbal preparations of Serapias lingua subsp. elsae, often in the form of teas or infusions, have been used to promote relaxation, reduce stress, and boost the immune system.
